SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class Memory

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.Memory
Direct Known Subclasses:
GetMainStorage
public class Memory extends Object
The Memory class represents a memory area that can be allocated, deallocated, and manipulated.

      Allocates a specified amount of memory and returns the address of the allocated memory.

      Returns:
      the address of the allocated memory

    • deallocate

      public void deallocate()
      Deallocates the memory.

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears the memory by setting all bytes to zero.

      public void set(byte b, int pos)
      Sets a byte at a specified position in the memory.

      pos - the position to set the byte at

    • get

      public byte get(int pos)
      Gets a byte from a specified position in the memory.
      Parameters:
      pos - the position to get the byte from
      Returns:
      the byte at the specified position

    • getSegment

      public byte[] getSegment(int offset, int len)
      Gets a segment of bytes from the memory.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position of the segment
      len - the length of the segment
      Returns:
      the byte array representing the segment

    • setSegment

      public void setSegment(byte[] src, int offset, int len)
      Sets a segment of bytes in the memory.
      Parameters:
      src - the source byte array
      offset - the starting position in the memory
      len - the length of the segment

    • setSegment

      public void setSegment(byte src, int offset, int len)
      Sets a segment of bytes in the memory to a specified value.
      Parameters:
      src - the byte value to set
      offset - the starting position in the memory
      len - the length of the segment

    • setSegment

      public void setSegment(byte[] src, int sourceOffset, int offset, int len)
      Sets a segment of bytes in the memory from a source byte array with specified offsets and length.
      Parameters:
      src - the source byte array
      sourceOffset - the starting position in the source array
      offset - the starting position in the memory
      len - the length of the segment

    • getCharSegment

      public char[] getCharSegment(int offset, int len)
      Gets a segment of characters from the memory.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position of the segment
      len - the length of the segment
      Returns:
      the character array representing the segment

    • dump

      public void dump(PrintStream o)
      Dumps the memory content to a specified PrintStream.
      Parameters:
      o - the PrintStream to dump the memory content to

    • contains

      public boolean contains(IDataArea a)
      Checks if the memory contains a specified data area.
      Parameters:
      a - the data area to check
      Returns:
      true if the memory contains the data area, false otherwise

    • getFootprint

      public int getFootprint()
      Returns the memory footprint. The memory footprint is a nibsort of identifier to be used with pointers, when required. It is added to the actual offset value stored in the underlying memory and subtracted from the value when returned by the `getOffset()` method. For example, a pointer addressing an area with offset 24 located in a memory object with footprint 3, will have an actual value of 27 stored in the pointer memory: - The `getOffset()` method of the pointer will return 24. - The `getInt()` method of the pointer will return 27. The reason for this is to have pointers which value is not zero when addressing special memory objects. By default, the memory footprint is zero. Special memory objects can override this method to return a different value.
      Returns:
      the footprint of the memory
